ogTitle: Pacific Pines vs Packers Camp: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pacific Pines and Packers Camp.
heading: Pacific Pines vs Packers Camp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pacific Pines, Queensland and Packers Camp, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pacific Pines has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Packers Camp (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pacific Pines or Packers Camp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pacific Pines has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Packers Camp scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pacific Pines scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Packers Camp scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pacific Pines or Packers Camp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pacific Pines has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
